title: Entanglement-Rank Duality in Quadratic Phase Quantum States
abstract: Absolutely Maximally Entangled (AME) states are important resources in quantum information processing; however, a general systematic approach for constructing these states remains a formidable challenge. We identify a finite-field rank structure underlying multipartite entanglement in a class of quadratic-phase quantum states defined by symmetric matrices over $\mathbb{F}_p$. We prove an exact Rank-Purity Duality: the Rényi-2 purity of any subsystem is determined solely by the rank of the phase matrix. Within this ansatz, the existence of an AME state is equivalent to the existence of a generating phase matrix $P$ whose bipartition submatrices are of full rank, reducing the condition for maximal entanglement to a rank constraint on $P$. This establishes a direct correspondence between entanglement and cut-rank geometry in finite-field matrices. Furthermore, for square-free local dimensions, we show that the entanglement structure factorises via the Chinese Remainder Theorem into independent prime-field contributions, yielding an exact additive decomposition of Rényi-2 entropies. These results provide an algebraic characterisation of entanglement in the quadratic phase formalism and enable the systematic construction of highly entangled states.
summary: The paper introduces an algebraic method to construct and verify highly entangled (AME) states by mapping entanglement properties to the rank of matrices over finite fields. By using the Chinese Remainder Theorem, the authors reduce the complexity of verifying large-scale systems from exponential to polynomial time. This allows for the explicit construction of massive entangled states that were previously computationally inaccessible.
main_problem: The difficulty of finding a systematic approach for constructing Absolutely Maximally Entangled (AME) states and the exponential complexity involved in verifying their entanglement properties.
main_result: The authors establish a Rank-Purity Duality that links the Rényi-2 purity of subsystems to the rank of submatrices in a phase matrix, enabling the efficient construction and certification of large-scale AME states like AME(17, 10001).
method: The study uses finite-field algebraic tools, including the Chinese Remainder Theorem for decomposing composite dimensions and a parallel tempering optimization algorithm to minimize rank deficits.
model_or_system: A class of multipartite quadratic-phase quantum states (including stabilizer states) defined by symmetric matrices over finite fields or rings.
key_observables: Rényi-2 purity, Rényi-2 entropy, and submatrix rank.
important_parameters: Number of parties (N), local dimension (d), and the rank of the phase matrix submatrices.
main_assumptions: The results primarily focus on the class of quadratic-phase quantum states and assume square-free dimensions for the CRT decomposition.
figures_summary: Figure 1 illustrates the CRT structure for square-free dimensions, showing how N-party states are represented across prime-sector states.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of AME construction, defines the quadratic-phase model, proves the Rank-Purity Duality and CRT decomposition, presents a numerical search algorithm, and demonstrates the successful construction and certification of a large-scale AME state.
why_interesting: This work provides a powerful algebraic framework for generating highly entangled states, which are essential resources for quantum error correction and quantum communication protocols.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. CRT structure for square-free d. N parties × r prime factors grid. Horizontal: CRT recombines a row to one d-level qudit. Vertical: each column is a prime-sector state.
